message:
perf: defer heavy visualizations with IntersectionObserver and optimize bundle loading

Reduce initial page load weight by deferring 19 visualization components
until they enter the viewport (with 600px rootMargin for preloading).
This avoids mounting all viz components eagerly on first render, which
was causing unnecessary JS execution and layout work on page load.

Key changes:
- app/page.tsx: add DeferredViz wrapper using useIntersectionObserver
  hook with triggerOnce; wrap all visualization sections so they render
  a skeleton placeholder until near-viewport; fix dynamic() loading
  props to use arrow functions returning JSX elements
- components/client-shell.tsx: lazy-load CustomCursor and SignalHUD
  with next/dynamic (ssr: false) instead of static imports, since they
  are client-only decorative components
- components/spec-evolution/viewer-loader.tsx: remove redundant
  QueryClientProvider wrapper (moved into viewer.tsx itself)
- components/spec-evolution/viewer.tsx: internalize QueryClientProvider,
  add proper TypeScript types for ECharts refs, pre-compute chart data
  with useMemo, batch-load all patches with getAllPatches() to eliminate
  N+1 async calls, add canvas-based evolution map background renderer
- next.config.ts: add turbopack.root for correct module resolution and
  compute projectRoot from import.meta.url

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
